name: What is a decision support system (DSS)? | Definition from TechTarget
snippet:
A decision support system (DSS) is a computer program used to improve a company's decision-making capabilities.
A decision support system (DSS) is a computer program used to improve a company's decision-making capabilities. It analyzes large amounts of data and presents an organization with the best possible options available.

name: Decision support system - Wikipedia
snippet: A decision support system (DSS) is an information system that supports business or organizational decision-making activities.

name: Decision Support Systems | Journal | ScienceDirect.com by Elsevier
snippet:
Read the latest articles of Decision Support Systems at ScienceDirect.com, Elsevier's leading platform of peer-reviewed scholarly literature.
The common thread of articles published in Decision Support Systems is their relevance to theoretical and technical issues in the support of enhanced decision making.

name: DSS (Decision Support System) is a computer system that assists in
snippet: DSS, or Decision Support System, is a computer system that assists in the adoption of business or organizational data collection and analytics methods.

name: The Role of Decision Support Systems (DSS) – exelisis
snippet: DSS is a computational application/software that processes different knowledge domains and provides recommendations to help users make informed decisions.

name: Decision support system – Knowledge and References
snippet: A decision support system (DSS) is a computer-based information system that assists decision makers in compiling and managing useful information.

name: Decision Support Systems - The Decision Lab
snippet: What is a Decision Support System? A decision support system (DSS) is a computer-based tool that helps individuals and organizations make better decisions by organizing data, analyzing information, and presenting it in a way that supports strategic or operational action.
